codeReview.summary: Decomposition target is met. FinishedGoodsWorkflowEngineService is reduced below the 500-line convention (459 LOC), while reservation/dispatch/slip/valuation behavior is moved into dedicated services (FinishedGoodsReservationEngine, FinishedGoodsDispatchEngine, PackagingSlipService, InventoryValuationService) and wiring preserves existing call surface through FinishedGoodsService. Redundant façade/delegate services were removed from the codebase and impacted tests were updated with gate-fast validation passing.

issues.description: Workflow engine still manually constructs extracted service collaborators in its constructor instead of relying on Spring dependency injection for each focused service bean. Behavior is correct, but this keeps orchestration wiring tightly coupled and less test-flexible.

sharedStateObservations.observation: When decomposition preserves external behavior, worker practice often updates existing tests rather than strict red-green TDD; backend-worker skill can explicitly allow this refactor-mode validation path.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature handoff documented a deliberate deviation from strict test-first due to behavior-preserving decomposition and reliance on regression suites plus gate-fast.

summary: PASS. Inventory workflow decomposition objectives (service split, engine size reduction, facade cleanup, and regression safety) are achieved.
